summary: Rev-5 rewrite structurally closes all three rev-2 BLOCKERs (A: ValidationError routing; B: direction-aware label gate; C: summarizer hash false-negative) and all five MAJOR groups. However, the Hypothesis property test cited as the closure mechanism for rev-2 BLOCKER_A (quality MAJOR-4) contains a silent path-traversal defect that three independent reviewers identified at the same line range. The test passes vacuously for any container-descent Sensitive annotation, structurally compromising the closure claim. Currently inert (all promoted models use flat-dict Sensitive), but listed in Phase 2 done-when criteria and cited as Phase 3's correctness foundation. One BLOCKER, two MAJOR, eight WARNINGS, seven MINOR.

BLOCKER_A_validation_error_routing.evidence: Task 4 routes via ToolArgumentError/ARG_ERROR; raise pattern verified at tools.py:2668-2801; catch/route verified at service.py:2480
BLOCKER_A_validation_error_routing.caveat: Closure mechanism (Task 19 property test) carries defect B1 — closure claim is structurally compromised until B1 fixed

BLOCKER_B_direction_aware_label_gate.evidence: Argument vs response paths now have distinct gate semantics in plan

BLOCKER_C_summarizer_hash_false_negative.evidence: Documented false-negative accepted as trade-off; surfaced explicitly rather than concealed

MAJOR_groups_1_to_5.evidence: All five groups have concrete addressing tasks in rev-5

blocking_issues.title: Hypothesis property test silently skips container-element Sensitive fields
blocking_issues.convergence: 3-way independent convergence at identical line range
blocking_issues.defect: Inner loop uses raw_value.get("items[*]")-style ad-hoc path parsing; returns None for any path containing [*] or {*}. Assertion `assert raw_value != redacted_value` evaluates None == None and passes vacuously.
why_blocking: Cited closure mechanism for rev-2 BLOCKER_A (quality MAJOR-4)
Listed in Phase 2 done-when criteria (plan line 1604)
Phase 3 commits to this test's correctness claim
Failure mode is silent (passing test, false confidence)
Inert today (all promoted models use flat-dict Sensitive) but defect is in shipped load-bearing security property test
blocking_issues.resolution: Replace ad-hoc path parsing with node.value_provider(instance) from existing TraversalNode API. ~10 lines.

major_issues.title: No oracle for iterator completeness
major_issues.issue: Walker and adequacy guard share the same iterator; shared-iterator design forecloses disagreement but not shared bugs. No mechanism flags a Sensitive annotation form walk_model_schema silently fails to descend into. Same structural failure shape as rev-2 BLOCKER B2 one level higher.
major_issues.resolution: Add separate-codepath oracle for currently-supported annotation forms (Annotated[scalar], Annotated[list], Annotated[dict], Optional[Annotated[scalar]]) asserting iterator yields expected set on a fixture model.

major_issues.title: Phase 3 call-order precondition is documentation-only
major_issues.issue: Contract 'call redact only after MANIFEST membership check' is required to prevent inversion converting Tier-3 quarantine to Tier-1 crash. No mechanical test in Phase 2 enforces the precondition. CLAUDE.md principle: 'if not mechanically enforced, assume next session won't know.'
major_issues.resolution: Add 5-line test in Task 17 calling redact_tool_call_arguments for a not-in-MANIFEST tool and asserting expected error class.

warnings.issue: Preflight gate cites '1565 tests collected'; actual ~15,022 (10x drift from earlier branch state)
warnings.fix: Refresh figure to current collection count

warnings.issue: Preflight gate cites '>=6 integration tests collected'; actual 781
TraversalNode.metadata: tuple[Any, ...] — tuple freeze-guard tool doesn't flag mutable _SensitiveMarker instances contained within
warnings.fix: Document design assumption inline (markers are not mutated; freeze elision is intentional)

warnings.issue: TDD discipline degrades from Task 5 onward ('Standard TDD task'); explicit failing-test-first protocol only spelled out for Tasks 1-4
warnings.fix: Add Preflight meta-rule that explicit step protocol applies to all tasks

warnings.issue: Task 15 (patch_*_options) outcome-dependent on Task 16 with no explicit cross-task bridge; cross-task memory is a known agentic failure mode
warnings.fix: Add explicit cross-task contract or merge tasks

warnings.issue: _summarize_set_source_options uses json.dumps on dict[str, Any] — raises TypeError on Pydantic-coerced datetime/bytes/UUID values, violating spec §9 RSK-03 'must not raise'

No per-entry runtime test for ~29-31 declarative manifest entries; adequacy guard catches internal consistency only. Highest risk: get_blob_content with sensitive_response_keys=('content',) if key mistyped
warnings.fix: Add per-entry runtime smoke test or parameterised test across all entries

warnings.issue: Missing test coverage: Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] scalar arm; partial-walker-failure atomicity; redact_tool_call_response with empty known_response_keys
warnings.fix: Add focused test cases for each

minor_issues.issue: Plan body inconsistent on '37 tools' vs '38 manifest entries' (request_advisor_hint adds one)
Notes tests/integration/web/composer/ doesn't exist; plan doesn't claim to create it — confirms reviewer awareness, not a defect
_TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal changes audit error_class from 'MissingRequiredPaths' to dispatch class; test_service.py:367 pins old string. Audit consumer impact not analysed
MANIFEST population pattern (20-commit incremental MappingProxyType rebinding) diverges from _build_tool_required_paths_index() factory precedent. Stylistic
Five rev-2 minors lack explicit closure tracking in spec §12.3 or plan Appendix B. m_dynamic_tool_name deserves warning comment in MANIFEST construction
Bulk editorial PR on declarative entries can flip 12+ hashes simultaneously, clearing single label-gate check. Documented as known limitation
Task 4 Step 4 _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal is conditional ('if redundant, remove'); should be unconditional — Pydantic model is strictly more capable

conflicts_resolved.justification: Three reviewers converged on the identical defect at the identical line range. Systems' framing wins on three structural grounds: (1) the test is the cited closure mechanism for a prior BLOCKER, so its compromise compromises that closure; (2) the test is on the Phase 2 done-when list; (3) the failure mode is silent. Quality and Architecture under-weighted the 'claims-closure-of-prior-BLOCKER' property. CLAUDE.md auditability standard ('confident wrong answer because we coerced garbage' applies analogously: a confident green test that exercises nothing is evidence tampering in the test corpus).
Speculative (no field currently known to be skipped) but structurally significant. Does not block; should be addressed before merge.
5-line mechanical-enforcement fix. CLAUDE.md mechanical-enforcement principle elevates above MINOR. Does not reach BLOCKER threshold because no current closure claim depends on it.

convergence_findings.treatment: Treated as ground truth; basis for the sole BLOCKER

recommendations.action: Replace ad-hoc path parsing in test_redaction_completeness_property.py:1479-1490 with node.value_provider(instance) from existing TraversalNode API
Add iterator-completeness oracle covering supported Sensitive annotation forms on a fixture model
Add 5-line MANIFEST-membership precondition test in Task 17
Replace json.dumps with model_dump_json or default=str in _summarize_set_source_options
Add per-entry runtime smoke tests for declarative manifest entries
Add Preflight meta-rule that explicit failing-test-first protocol applies to all tasks (not only 1-4)
After B1 fix, rev-4 review can be focused re-check of the single test file rather than full pass

next_steps.rev4_review_scope: Focused re-check of test_redaction_completeness_property.py:1479-1490 after B1 patch
